title: tests/serve/serve.go: use a kernel-assigned port
body:
#### What type of PR is this?

/kind flake

#### What this PR does / why we need it:

In integration tests, when firing up a minimal server to serve up content so that we can retrieve it using HTTP, make it possible to let the kernel assign us which port to use, so that the script that's calling us doesn't have to make a(n occasionally bad) guess.

#### How to verify it

Tests should continue to pass.

#### Which issue(s) this PR fixes:

None

#### Special notes for your reviewer:

Sample error log at https://cirrus-ci.com/task/6625704805662720?logs=integration_test#L871.

#### Does this PR introduce a user-facing change?

```release-note
None
```

comments.body: Oh, right, `usleep` isn't standard, and GNU `sleep` has accepted floating point numbers for some time now.

@edsantiago a quick eyeball would be appreciated.
Changes LGTM, but one of the tests isn't happy and it looks like you may need a rebase.

The pull request process is described [here](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/owners.md#the-code-review-process)
Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: - ~~[OWNERS](https://github.com/containers/buildah/blob/main/OWNERS)~~ [giuseppe,nalind] Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment


> One of the [failures](https://api.cirrus-ci.com/v1/task/5278661495488512/logs/integration_test.log) looks real:
> 
> ```
> [+0583s] ok 198 bud with Containerfile and Dockerfile
>          not ok 199 bud-http-context-with-Containerfile
>          # (from function `starthttpd' in file ./helpers.bash, line 50,
>          #  from function `_test_http' in file ./bud.bats, line 680,
>          #  in test file ./bud.bats, line 2144)
>          #   `_test_http http-context-containerfile context.tar' failed with status 2
>          # /var/tmp/go/src/github.com/containers/buildah/tests /var/tmp/go/src/github.com/containers/buildah/tests
>          # # command-line-arguments
>          # ../go/src/github.com/containers/buildah/tests/serve/serve.go:50:16: undefined: os.CreateTemp
>          # /var/tmp/go/src/github.com/containers/buildah/tests /var/tmp/go/src/github.com/containers/buildah/tests
> ```

Oh, `os.CreateTemp()` was new in Go 1.16.  Switching to `io/ioutil.TempFile()`, which has been around longer.

> The other failure I'm seeing looks flakey, but looks familiar: it's the `FROM AS` one. Wasn't that fixed recently?
> 
> [Example 1](https://api.cirrus-ci.com/v1/task/5841611448909824/logs/integration_test.log):
> 
> ```
> [+0433s] not ok 146 bud with FROM AS construct with layers
>          # (from function `die' in file ./helpers.bash, line 249,
>          #  from function `run_buildah' in file ./helpers.bash, line 236,
>          #  in test file ./bud.bats, line 1471)

>          # # [checking for: docker.io/library/alpine]
>          # # [restoring from cache: /var/tmp/buildah-image-cache.6337 / docker.io/library/alpine]
>          # Getting image source signatures
>          # Copying blob sha256:9d16cba9fb961d1aafec9542f2bf7cb64acfc55245f9e4eb5abecd4cdc38d749
>          # Copying config sha256:961769676411f082461f9ef46626dd7a2d1e2b2a38e6a44364bcbecf51e66dd4
>          # Writing manifest to image destination
>          # Storing signatures
>          # $ buildah bud --layers --signature-policy /var/tmp/go/src/github.com/containers/buildah/tests/./policy.json -t test1 /var/tmp/go/src/github.com/containers/buildah/tests/./bud/from-as
>          # [1/2] STEP 1/4: FROM alpine AS base
>          # [1/2] STEP 2/4: RUN touch /1
>          # --> 903fe4e8486
>          # [1/2] STEP 3/4: ENV LOCAL=/1
>          # --> 108af66450e
>          # [1/2] STEP 4/4: RUN find $LOCAL
>          # /1
>          # --> 38b78a482f6
>          # [2/2] STEP 1/2: FROM base
>          # Resolving "base" using unqualified-search registries (/var/tmp/go/src/github.com/containers/buildah/tests/./registries.conf)
>          # Trying to pull docker.io/library/base:latest...
>          # Trying to pull quay.io/base:latest...
>          # Trying to pull registry.fedoraproject.org/base:latest...
>          # error creating build container: 3 errors occurred while pulling:
>          #  * initializing source docker://base:latest: reading manifest latest in quay.io/libpod/base: unauthorized: access to the requested resource is not authorized
>          #  * initializing source docker://quay.io/base:latest: reading manifest latest in quay.io/base: StatusCode: 404, <!DOCTYPE HTML PUBLIC "-//W3C//DTD HTML 3.2 Final/...
>          #  * initializing source docker://registry.fedoraproject.org/base:latest: reading manifest latest in registry.fedoraproject.org/base: manifest unknown: manifest unknown
>          # [ rc=125 (** EXPECTED 0 **) ]
>```

There was a short window where after a stage completed, we hadn't recorded the image ID of the stage's final product.  A different stage that was waiting for the first stage to complete might have not been able to retrieve that image ID, and fallen back to assuming that the name of the stage was the name of an image that it needed to pull.  That window should be closed now.

> That window should be closed now.

Hmm. Any idea what those flakes are about, then?

> > That window should be closed now.
> 
> Hmm. Any idea what those flakes are about, then?

#3393 should have gotten most of it, this added a tweak to remove that window.
